name: counterexample-search
triggers: The task asks whether a property holds universally ('for all', 'in general', 'prove that', 'does it always/ever')
The draft asserts a universal or impossibility claim ('always', 'never', 'all', 'every', 'guaranteed', 'impossible', 'cannot happen')
counterindications: The claim is already scoped to observations ('in the 30 cases tested', 'so far', 'in our data'); nothing universal is asserted
The property is definitional or machine-enforced (tautology, compiler-checked type guarantee); a counterexample search is vacuous
